patch.value: Export: dry-run default + upsert existing issues by provenance

Change exporter so it DEFAULTS to dry-run (print would-create/update). Real writes only with --apply/--no-dry-run AND --repo. Upsert: if a pm item's provenance tag points at an existing issue in target repo, PATCH it instead of creating a duplicate.
dry-run default verified, writes opt-in only

Export now SAFE BY DEFAULT (dry-run). Verified: 'pm github export --repo X' prints would-create/update plan + writes NOTHING (dry_run:true); --json returns plan object (no stdout corruption); items imported from the target repo correctly show would_update:34 (upsert via provenance), a different repo shows would_create:34; '--apply' without --repo errors safely (no writes); '--apply' requires token+repo. Real GitHub writes NOT performed (constraint). Legacy --push kept as --apply alias. Also fixed upsert reader bug (pm list -> list-all so closed items aren't duplicated).
